key_insight: A GNN trained only on finite Cayley graphs can transfer learned algebraic and geometric cues to truncated Cayley graphs of infinite finitely generated groups.
engineering_relevance: This suggests graph models can infer large-scale group growth behavior from local connectivity patterns, enabling scalable analysis of algebraic structure from finite samples.
method: Train and validate on finite complete and truncated Cayley graphs only
Test zero-shot on truncated Cayley graphs of unseen infinite groups
Evaluate across free abelian, Heisenberg, dihedral, free, and product-group families
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong zero-shot generalization on multiple infinite-group families using truncated Cayley graphs without retraining.
watch_item: Because the model sees only truncated graphs, performance may depend on truncation scale and may not fully capture asymptotic growth beyond local neighborhoods.
paper_title: From Finite Cayley Graphs to Growth of Infinite Groups
